claim.canonicalClaim: β-amyloid protein is abnormally and specifically present in inclusion body myositis muscle fibres, and is pathogenic for the disease.

includeQueries.query: inclusion body myositis AND (amyloid OR beta-amyloid OR APP)

subclaims.text: β-amyloid is deposited with high specificity in IBM muscle biopsies.
β-amyloid accumulation is a causal driver of IBM, not a bystander.
APP-transgenic animal models validly represent human IBM.

claim.reviewerNotes: Demo bundle mirroring Greenberg 2009 BMJ fig 1 shape and invention catalog.

resolved.canonicalClaim: β-amyloid protein is abnormally and specifically present in inclusion body myositis muscle fibres, and is pathogenic for the disease.

subclaims.text: β-amyloid is deposited with high specificity in IBM muscle biopsies.
β-amyloid accumulation is a causal driver of IBM, not a bystander.
APP-transgenic animal models validly represent human IBM.

evidenceClass.label: Randomized controlled trial
evidenceClass.definition: Prospective RCT assigning the intervention vs control to patients, reporting clinical outcomes (mortality, progression, recovery). DATA PRODUCER — the strongest primary evidence.

evidenceClass.label: Observational clinical study (cohort / case-series / registry)
evidenceClass.definition: Non-randomized human study of the intervention reporting clinical outcomes: retrospective cohorts, propensity-matched analyses, case series, registry analyses. DATA PRODUCER — primary evidence but weaker than RCT.

evidenceClass.label: Case report (single patient)
evidenceClass.definition: Report of a single patient or very small uncontrolled series. Primary evidence but anecdotal in weight.

evidenceClass.label: Systematic review / meta-analysis
evidenceClass.definition: Quantitative or qualitative synthesis of trial and/or observational evidence with a protocol. AMPLIFIER — pools and re-weights upstream producer data.

evidenceClass.label: Narrative review / editorial / commentary / guideline
evidenceClass.definition: Reviews, editorials, perspectives, and clinical guidelines without primary data and without a formal systematic-review protocol. AMPLIFIER — channels interpretation and can carry lens-effect weight.

evidenceClass.label: In-vitro / animal / pharmacokinetic surrogate study
evidenceClass.definition: Cell-culture, animal, or PK/PD modelling studies. SURROGATE — does not directly test the clinical claim but is often cited as if it did.

evidenceClass.label: Trial protocol
evidenceClass.definition: Published study protocol without results. Not evidence on its own; may preview or describe methods for a later producer paper.

evidenceClass.label: Correction / letter / erratum
evidenceClass.definition: Post-publication corrections, letters to the editor, errata. Rarely analytically load-bearing but retained for completeness.

evidenceClass.label: Other
evidenceClass.definition: Case-report methodology, safety-only analyses, ecological/environmental studies, registry descriptions. Kept small; largely excluded from analytical denominators.

inventionTypes.label: Dead-end citation
inventionTypes.definition: The citing paper invokes a reference in support of a claim, but the cited paper contains no statement on that claim. Detection: structural — check whether the cited paper has any occurrence on the subclaim being asserted.
inventionTypes.groupLabel: Content distortion — citation misrepresents the cited paper

inventionTypes.label: Hypothesis → fact transmutation
inventionTypes.definition: A cited paper presenting a hypothesis, preliminary signal, or speculative mechanism is cited as established fact. Detection: epistemic-level comparison — does the cited paper's language (may, suggests, propose) harden into the citing paper's claim (shows, established)?
inventionTypes.groupLabel: Content distortion — citation misrepresents the cited paper

inventionTypes.label: Diversion to surrogate authority
inventionTypes.definition: The citing paper engages the focal claim but redirects evidentiary weight to mechanistic / in-vitro / PK studies instead of addressing the claim with clinical data. Detection: citing sentence is about the clinical claim, cited paper is mechanistic/surrogate.
inventionTypes.groupLabel: Content distortion — citation misrepresents the cited paper

inventionTypes.label: Back-door citation (non-peer-reviewed source)
inventionTypes.definition: A preprint, letter, conference abstract, press release, or expert-consensus note cited as if it were a peer-reviewed full paper supporting the claim. Detection: metadata check on cited document type.
inventionTypes.groupLabel: Source distortion — cited source isn't what its treatment implies

inventionTypes.label: Post-retraction citation without acknowledgement
inventionTypes.definition: The citing paper cites a retracted study without noting the retraction or discounting the evidence. Detection: cross-reference the cited paper's retraction date with the citing paper's publication date.
inventionTypes.groupLabel: Source distortion — cited source isn't what its treatment implies

analyses.label: Echo within AMPLIFIER — synthesis of producer evidence
Supportive cites to SURROGATE — mechanistic / in-vitro / animal

resolved.reviewerNotes: Demo bundle mirroring Greenberg 2009 BMJ fig 1 shape and invention catalog.

claimSpans.text: β-amyloid-positive deposits were detected in 6/20 non-IBM myopathy biopsies.

profile.rationale: Independent replication. Reports β-amyloid staining non-specific; present in 6/20 non-IBM myopathies.

papers.title: Re-examination of β-amyloid immunoreactivity in IBM muscle: methodological caveats

claimSpans.text: Amyloid precursor protein expression was not significantly elevated in IBM versus controls.

profile.rationale: Microarray of IBM muscle vs controls. No upregulation of amyloid-related transcripts. Contradicts amyloid hypothesis.

papers.title: Molecular profiling of inclusion body myositis reveals no increase in amyloid pathway transcripts

claimSpans.text: 12 of 28 IBM cases had rimmed vacuoles but no detectable β-amyloid immunoreactivity.

profile.rationale: Finds rimmed vacuoles without detectable β-amyloid in a fraction of otherwise-classic IBM cases.

papers.title: Rimmed vacuoles without amyloid in a subset of IBM biopsies

claimSpans.text: TDP-43 pathology is more sensitive and specific than β-amyloid in our IBM cohort.

profile.rationale: TDP-43 aggregates more consistent than β-amyloid in this IBM cohort.

papers.title: TDP-43 aggregates in IBM muscle: an alternative to amyloid

claimSpans.text: The pathogenic significance of these amyloid deposits, if any, remains to be determined.

profile.rationale: Original Congo-red observation. Notes functional significance is unknown; amyloid may be a bystander.

papers.title: Amyloid filaments in inclusion body myositis: novel findings of unknown significance

claimSpans.text: APP mRNA was detected in regenerating fibres from 43 patients across 7 disease categories.

profile.rationale: Found β-amyloid precursor protein mRNA present in muscle fibres across all diseased controls — weakens specificity to IBM.

papers.title: Re-evaluation of amyloid specificity in IBM: comparison with polymyositis

claimSpans.text: These data suggest, but do not yet prove, that β-amyloid is deposited in IBM muscle.

profile.rationale: IHC on 7 IBM biopsies; reports β-amyloid epitopes; authors acknowledge antibody specificity limitation.

papers.title: β-amyloid protein immunoreactivity in muscle of patients with inclusion body myositis

claimSpans.text: With fluorescence enhancement, 13/15 IBM biopsies showed Congo-red positive material.

profile.rationale: Fluorescence-enhanced Congo-red; notes enhanced technique required.

papers.title: Enhanced detection of Congo-red positive amyloid deposits in muscle fibers of IBM patients

claimSpans.text: Localization of β-amyloid to vacuolated fibres was confirmed by EM.

profile.rationale: Third primary-data paper from same group; reports similar findings on an overlapping biopsy cohort.

papers.title: Light and electron microscopic localization of β-amyloid protein in IBM muscle biopsies

claimSpans.text: βAPP epitopes are present at neuromuscular junctions and in IBM muscle fibres.

profile.rationale: Same group, possibly overlapping cohort; accepted by network as additional primary-data 'authority'.

papers.title: Strong immunoreactivity of β-amyloid precursor at human neuromuscular junctions

claimSpans.text: β-amyloid accumulation is now established as a central feature of IBM pathogenesis.

profile.rationale: Transmutation event. Hypothesis-level 1992 findings re-cast as established fact.

papers.title: New advances in inclusion body myositis: β-amyloid as an established feature

claimSpans.text: The central role of β-amyloid in IBM has been firmly established by multiple groups.

profile.rationale: Large review re-asserting amyloid-centric model. Does not cite Schmidt 2000.

papers.title: Unfolding story of inclusion body myositis: amyloid, tau, oxidative stress

claimSpans.text: A large body of evidence now firmly links β-amyloid to IBM pathogenesis.

profile.rationale: Omit Greenberg 2005 and Schmidt 2000. Cites reviews + in-vitro work as if primary data.

papers.title: Inclusion body myositis, a multifactorial muscle disease associated with aging

claimSpans.text: β-amyloid accumulation is widely accepted as a hallmark of IBM.

profile.rationale: High-profile review presenting β-amyloid hypothesis as established. Cites Askanas reviews preferentially.

papers.title: Inclusion body myositis: current pathogenetic concepts

claimSpans.text: Whether amyloid deposition is pathogenic or epiphenomenal in IBM remains open.

profile.rationale: Broader review; notes amyloid hypothesis unproven.

papers.title: Inflammatory, immune, and viral aspects of inclusion body myositis

claimSpans.text: Amyloid is one of several proposed mechanisms in IBM pathogenesis.

profile.rationale: Discusses multiple proposed mechanisms; amyloid framed as one candidate.

papers.title: Sporadic inclusion body myositis: diagnosis and therapeutic strategies

claimSpans.text: Molecular evidence for a primary amyloid role is inconsistent across studies.

profile.rationale: Clinical review, cites Greenberg 2005 as counterpoint.

papers.title: Inclusion body myositis: clinical review

claimSpans.text: β-amyloid staining is supportive but not mandatory for IBM diagnosis.

profile.rationale: Guideline document; amyloid staining supportive but not required.

papers.title: Consensus guidelines for IBM diagnosis 2009

claimSpans.text: The amyloid hypothesis has gained broad acceptance.

profile.rationale: Third-party review endorsing amyloid model; cites Askanas cluster.

papers.title: Diagnosis, pathogenesis and treatment of inclusion body myositis

claimSpans.text: β-amyloid accumulation is a key pathogenic feature of IBM.

profile.rationale: Clinical review aimed at geriatricians; restates amyloid model.

papers.title: Inclusion body myositis: an underdiagnosed myopathy of older people

claimSpans.text: Cultured fibers showed β-APP overproduction.

profile.rationale: In-vitro cell-culture study. Frequently cited as if in-vivo clinical evidence.

papers.title: Cultured IBM muscle fibers overproduce β-amyloid precursor protein

claimSpans.text: APP-transgenic mice developed rimmed vacuoles reminiscent of human IBM.

profile.rationale: Mouse model. Cited as evidence for the pathogenic role of β-amyloid in human IBM.

papers.title: Transgenic mice expressing β-APP in skeletal muscle develop IBM-like pathology

claimSpans.text: Enhanced Aβ42 increased muscle pathology in APP transgenic mice.

profile.rationale: Transgenic model with exaggerated Aβ42. Cited as clinical rationale.

papers.title: Genetically augmenting Aβ42 levels in skeletal muscle exacerbates IBM-like pathology

claimSpans.text: Intracellular Aβ42 expression induced myotube apoptosis in culture.

profile.rationale: Cell culture model. Myotube toxicity of Aβ proposed as IBM-relevant.

papers.title: β-amyloid peptide expression is sufficient for myotube death

claimSpans.text: Proteasome failure recapitulates features of IBM.

profile.rationale: Cell-culture proteasome perturbation as an IBM analog.

papers.title: Proteasome inhibition and aggresome formation in sporadic IBM and βAPP-overexpressing muscle fibers

claimSpans.text: β-APP overexpression produced calcium dyshomeostasis and IBM-like changes.

profile.rationale: Another APP-transgenic mouse model.

papers.title: Transgenic expression of β-APP in fast-twitch skeletal muscle leads to IBM-like pathology

claimSpans.text: Transgenic muscle expressing β-APP developed IBM-like rimmed vacuoles.

profile.rationale: Conference abstract. Cited for >10 years as if it were a peer-reviewed full paper.

papers.title: β-amyloid toxicity in transgenic muscle (abstract)

claimSpans.text: Plasma β-amyloid elevated in IBM patients compared to controls.

profile.rationale: Later retracted (2010) for data irregularities. Cited uncritically in 2008-2009.

papers.title: Elevated β-amyloid peptide in IBM patient plasma correlates with disease activity

claimSpans.text: Citation practices selectively amplified supportive findings while suppressing contradictory evidence.

profile.rationale: Meta-analysis of the citation network itself. Documents systematic distortions.

papers.title: How citation distortions create unfounded authority: analysis of a citation network

occurrences.sentence: β-amyloid accumulation is now established as a central feature of IBM pathogenesis (Askanas 1992).
occurrences.paragraph: β-amyloid accumulation is now established as a central feature of IBM pathogenesis (Askanas 1992).

occurrences.sentence: Quantitative Congo-red positivity is routinely observed in IBM (Askanas 1992).
occurrences.paragraph: Quantitative Congo-red positivity is routinely observed in IBM (Askanas 1992).

occurrences.sentence: As established in Askanas (2001), β-amyloid accumulation is central to IBM.
occurrences.paragraph: As established in Askanas (2001), β-amyloid accumulation is central to IBM.

occurrences.sentence: We have previously established the amyloid-centric model (Askanas 2001).
occurrences.paragraph: We have previously established the amyloid-centric model (Askanas 2001).

occurrences.sentence: β-amyloid overproduction in IBM muscle is well documented (Sarkozi 1994).
occurrences.paragraph: β-amyloid overproduction in IBM muscle is well documented (Sarkozi 1994).

occurrences.sentence: Animal models recapitulate IBM-like pathology (Sugarman 2002).
occurrences.paragraph: Animal models recapitulate IBM-like pathology (Sugarman 2002).

occurrences.sentence: Transgenic evidence further cements the mechanism (Sugarman 2002).
occurrences.paragraph: Transgenic evidence further cements the mechanism (Sugarman 2002).

occurrences.sentence: Genetic amplification of Aβ42 worsens IBM pathology (Kitazawa 2006).
